background.explorerFinding: Edax's 47-feature (13-kind) pattern set differs from the current production v5 (V3 = 38 instances + 2 scalar features) mainly in three ways: (1) corner 2x5 blocks (corner5x2, already implemented in T087 ablation but never adopted), (2) short diagonal-4 (diag4, previously unimplemented), (3) a constant bias term (previously unimplemented).
background.priorPrecedent: B3 scalar features were rejected in T158 but adopted in T166 once re-evaluated under the new regime (Egaroucid full corpus + D4 canonical + early stopping); this task re-evaluates corner5x2/diag4/constant under the same new regime.

engineChanges: engine/src/patterns.rs: PatternConfig::V3Corner5x2 (D1, 46 instances/11 classes) reuses existing corner5x2_patterns(); PatternConfig::V3Corner5x2Diag4 (D2, 50 instances/12 classes) adds new diag4_patterns() built the same way as diagonal_offset_patterns (symmetry_orbit-based, no hand-written cell lists).

engine/src/pattern_eval.rs: ScalarFeatureKind::Constant (value always 1, scale_shift=0) added; new ALL_SCALAR_FEATURE_KINDS constant centralizes the kind list used by score(), with_zeroed_scalar_features(), and the PWV4/PWV6 scalar-feature-count validation (bumped from 2 to 3).
trainerChanges: train/src/bin/train_patterns_v3.rs: t158_config generalized into scalar_feature_config(name, pattern_config, scalar_features); new configs t168-d1 (V3Corner5x2 + mobility/exposure) and t168-d2 (V3Corner5x2Diag4 + mobility/exposure/constant).

existingPathInvariance.method: git stash the T168 diff, rebuild release, train an existing config (t158-b3-canonical, 20000-record simple-corpus subset), record SHA-256; git stash pop, rebuild, re-run the identical command.

matrix.data: Egaroucid full corpus (--simple-corpus, --simple-max-records unset), identical to T165 (train_samples=21210114)

t165Comparison.reason: Same data, same split (position-hash), same flags as T165 configs B/C, so frozen MAE is directly comparable per the pre-registered rule.

preregisteredSelection.rule: final candidate = smallest frozen MAE among configs that improve over T165 candidate C (4.702778); withdraw (no candidate) if none improve

healthChecks.method: throwaway test engine/tests/t168_health_check.rs (deleted after use); loaded each of the 6 actual trained .bin files, sampled ~440 positions per file from 40 self-play games, verified D4-invariance (tolerance 1e-2) and finiteness of all weights.
healthChecks.divergenceCheck: grepped all *.metrics.tsv for nan/inf: none found

sizeMeasurements.note: D1 raw delta matches the task's +13.7MB estimate closely. Not treated as a blocker per task instructions.

npsReference.method: throwaway bench engine/tests/t168_nps_reference.rs (deleted after use); zero-coefficient canonical models, stratified 8-position corpus from bench/edax-compare/t158a_engine_cost_positions.json, score()-only microbenchmark, run in isolation after all 6 training runs completed.
npsReference.note: Reference only; the real gate is T169's actual game-play NPS impact.

comparisonBaseline.note: current production weights (T165 candidate C, T166-gated, T167-deployed)

notes.metricsJsonConfigField: Same caveat as T165: the 'config' field inside *-earlystop.metrics.json is the bare TrainingConfig.name (t168-d1/t168-d2) and does not reflect --canonical; canonical-ness is read from the filename's run_name suffix and magic bytes (PWV6).
notes.d2VsD1: D2 (diag4 + constant added on top of D1) did not improve over D1 in this run; its best seed (4.521531) is worse than D1's best (4.492196) though still better than T165 C. Root-cause attribution (diag4 vs constant, or early-stop timing/overfitting at this training scale) was not investigated further; out of scope for this task.
